page.title: Export Deliverables
page.subtitle: Gather all the deliverables (GitHub repositories) you built in this project in one place and download them bundled as a handoff ZIP file.
page.guide_btn_title: Show the Deliverables page guide again

confirm_delete.title: Delete Repository
confirm_delete.message:
{url}

The registration info will be deleted (the actual GitHub repo is not affected).

readiness.title: Handoff Readiness
readiness.desc: 7 items checked automatically — when all are ✅, you can hand the project off to the next team as-is.

has_repo.label: Repository registered
has_repo.hint_fail: Register at least one GitHub repository
has_repo.why: Without a repository there is no code to hand off. It's the very first foundation you need.

lint_avg.label: Average code check ≥ 80%
lint_avg.why: 80% or higher signals the code follows common conventions. Below that, the receiving team needs more time to adapt.

lineage_coverage.label: Lineage mapping ≥ 80%
lineage_coverage.hint_none: Not analyzed yet — run "Analyze" in the deliverable lineage above
lineage_coverage.why: Strong "requirements ↔ design ↔ code" links let the next team quickly find where to make changes.

readme.label: README in every repository
readme.why: Without a README, day one of handoff stalls at "how do I even start this?". A getting-started guide is the first impression of trust.

license.label: LICENSE in every repository
license.why: Without a license, public release and redistribution are legally unclear. An explicit license is essential for the receiving team to use it with confidence.

activity.label: Commit activity within the last 30 days
activity.why: More than a month without commits signals "abandoned code." Being up to date at handoff is a key indicator of handoff readiness.

prd.label: Requirements (PRD) exist
prd.hint_fail: Create a PRD first on the Plan screen
prd.why: When "what to build" is documented, the next team can continue work in the same direction.

repo_grid.title: Repositories by Role
repo_grid.empty_desc: Repositories are registered automatically when you enter a GitHub URL on the Code · Lint screens, or you can add them directly with the button above.

lang_stack.title: Language / Tech Stack Distribution
lang_stack.desc: Sums the language ratios across all registered repositories. See at a glance what tech the receiving team needs to know.
lang_stack.empty: No language data to show yet. Add a GitHub repository and the language breakdown will appear automatically.

lineage.title: Did my plan all make it into the code?
lineage.desc: AI checks at a glance whether the features (scenarios) you planned actually made it into the code.

empty.value_title: Connect your code and we'll auto-check whether everything you planned got built
empty.value_desc: Connect a GitHub repo and run the check — we compare your planned features against the actual code files and pinpoint what's missing.
empty.ready_desc: Press Check and AI compares your plan ↔ code (about 1–2 min).

result.headline: {done} / {total} planned scenarios confirmed in code
result.headline_none: No user scenarios in your plan yet — fill in the PRD first.
result.all_done: All planned scenarios are in the code 🎉
result.advanced_toggle: Advanced (for developers) — detailed mapping · quality · history

analyzing.title: AI is analyzing deliverable lineage
analyzing.desc: Mapping by combining {count} Repo file trees with Design deliverables.

zero_match.title: Analysis completed but matched 0 items
zero_match.desc_html: To keep the AI from hallucinating code, only paths that actually exist in the fileTree are included in the results. One of the following is likely the cause:
zero_match.reason1_html: Deliverable names are Korean while code is English → keyword match fails (e.g., "사용자 인증 서비스" ↔ UserAuthService.ts)
zero_match.reason2: Could not fetch the registered Repo's fileTree (private repo or GitHub rate limit)
zero_match.reason3: Matching keywords are too generic ("Service", "Manager", etc.)
zero_match.tip: Or register ground-truth labels via each deliverable's ✏️ button to measure matching accuracy.

quality.title: Match quality (labels {labeled}/{total})
quality.empty: ℹ No deliverables have ground-truth labels registered. Register candidate files via each item's ✏️ button to measure match quality.

error.desc_rate_limit: Unauthenticated calls are limited to 60 per hour. The cache expires automatically after 1 hour, so please retry.
error.desc_generic: Please check that the repository is public and the URL is correct.

obsidian.home_intro: This vault was generated by Harness. Open the graph view (Ctrl/Cmd+G) to see your plan's structure at a glance.
obsidian.empty_node_warn: This item is still empty — you can fill it with Harness's 'Fill with AI'.
obsidian.readme_intro: This folder is a set of plan & design notes you can open directly in Obsidian.
obsidian.readme_step2: In Obsidian, choose 'Open folder as vault' and select this folder
obsidian.readme_step3: Open the graph view (Ctrl/Cmd+G) to see your plan's structure
obsidian.readme_note: This vault is a one-way snapshot at export time. Edits here do not flow back to Harness. When re-exporting, unzip into a new folder.

handoff.title: Handoff Package
handoff.desc: Bundles the deliverables into a ZIP for download, tailored to the target role (developer · PM · architect).
handoff.note: ⚠️ Source code is not included — clone the actual code directly from the repo URLs. This ZIP is a document package containing only design specs, reports, and links.
handoff.dev_aria: Download developer package: design specs · ERD · coding rules · PRD · Lint
handoff.dev_content: Design specs + ERD + coding rules (skills) + PRD + Lint
handoff.pm_aria: Download PM package: PRD · CPS · requirements traceability · open questions · Lint
handoff.pm_content: PRD + context (CPS) + requirements traceability + open questions + Lint
handoff.architect_aria: Download architect package: design specs · ERD · design traceability · open questions · Lint
handoff.architect_content: Design specs + ERD + design traceability + open questions + Lint
handoff.obsidian_aria: Download Obsidian Vault: plan & design as wikilinked notes
handoff.obsidian_content: Your whole plan & design as [[wikilinked]] notes — Obsidian graph view ready

zip.obsidian_no_data: No plan/design data to export yet. Run Plan (01) and Design (02) first.
zip.note_html:
> ⚠️ **Source code is not included in this package.** Clone the actual code directly from the
> Repository URLs above. This ZIP is a document package containing only design specs, reports, and links.
zip.role_for_dev: For: developers (the people who will write the code from this spec)
zip.role_for_pm: For: product managers (the people who will track & manage the product)
zip.role_for_architect: For: system architects (the people who will review the design)
zip.about_dev: A package for developers to write code from this spec. It includes the design specs, the data model (ERD), the coding rules (skills) to follow, and the current code quality (Lint).
zip.about_pm: A package for product managers to understand what was decided, how far it has progressed, and what is left. It includes the PRD, context (CPS), requirements traceability, and open questions.
zip.about_architect: A package for system architects to review the design as a whole. It includes the design specs, the data model (ERD), design rationale traceability, open questions, and the current state of the code (Lint).
zip.first_dev: 1) Read `02_plan/prd.md` to understand what to build → 2) Check the data & structure with the `03_design/` specs and `erd.xlsx` → 3) Implement while following the coding rules in `skills/`. Don't decide ⚠ markers and the open questions in `06_review/open-questions.md` on your own — confirm them first.
zip.first_pm: 1) Confirm the product direction with `02_plan/prd.md` and `cps.md` → 2) Check whether the requirements made it into the design (coverage) with `07_traceability/lineage.xlsx` → 3) Drive the next decisions using the open questions in `06_review/open-questions.md`.
zip.first_architect: 1) Get a full view of the design from SPACK · DDD · Architecture in `03_design/` → 2) Review the data model with `erd.xlsx` and the design rationale with `07_traceability/lineage.xlsx` → 3) Check the risks using the open questions in `06_review/open-questions.md` and the current state of the code in `05_lint/`.
zip.open_questions_body: These are items the design hasn't decided yet. Read `06_review/open-questions.md` and lock them down before you build the code.
zip.file_erd: Data model ERD — entities · attributes · relationships (Excel)
zip.file_open_questions: List of open questions — must be decided before implementation

handoff.empty_hint: Add a GitHub repository first (in the repositories area above) to download the packages below.

guide.headline: Final Artifacts & Handoff
guide.sub: Gather all deliverables (Repo · specs · Lint results · coding rules) in one place, review them via KPIs, and hand them off bundled into a single ZIP.

step1.title: Deliverables Overview
step1.subtitle: Final Artifacts
step1.desc: Organizes all of the project's deliverables (GitHub repositories) on one screen. Shows the finished results from Plan → Design → Code → Lint via KPIs and lineage, and in the final stage you can bundle them into a handoff ZIP.
step1.tip: This is the "done" stage — the final deliverables gateway you hand off to external stakeholders or the next team.

step2.title: Register Repositories
step2.subtitle: Frontend · Backend · DB · Mobile · Other
step2.desc: Use the "+ Repository" button to register the project's GitHub repos by role. After registration, metadata (stars, forks, recent commits, language distribution) and lint scores are fetched automatically and summed into the KPI cards.
step2.tip: Even if one project has multiple repos (e.g., front + back + infra), they all gather in one place. Deleting registration info does not affect the GitHub original.

step3.title: Lineage & Quality
step3.subtitle: PRD → Design → Code Traceability
step3.desc: Shows traceability metrics for which PRD Story / Design spec each code node derives from. Provides four visualization tools: ground-truth label import, coverage badge, diff, and the full lineage graph.
step3.tip: The AVG LINT % card turns red below 60% and green at 80% or above — a quality signal at a glance.

step4.title: Handoff ZIP — Hand Off in One Click
step4.subtitle: One-click Delivery
step4.desc: Bundles all repo info, specs (SPACK · DDD · Architecture), lint results, and coding rules into a single ZIP. The recipient just unzips it to instantly access every deliverable — no extra permission setup, hand off right away.
step4.tip: Telling the recipient "just start with 0_START_HERE.md" is all the guidance they need.

quickstart.title: New here? It only takes 3 steps
quickstart.step1_desc: Register your GitHub repositories by role.
quickstart.step2_desc: Use AI to verify your planned features made it into the code.
quickstart.step3_desc: Bundle design specs & reports into a ZIP to hand off to the next team.
